title: Cerasus × fukudana
taxonomy: Cerasus × fukudana is a hybrid species, specifically a cross between two species of Cerasus, a genus of flowering plants in the family Rosaceae. The classification of Cerasus × fukudana is within the genus Cerasus and the family Rosaceae. This hybrid is not typically classified as a separate genus. Cerasus × fukudana is a cherry tree hybrid and is classified based on its parent species. It is often cultivated for its ornamental value and fruit production.

intro: Cerasus × fukudana, also known as Japanese apricot or Fukudana cherry, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The health benefits of Cerasus × fukudana include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat, as well as its antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cerasus × fukudana is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory infections and promote overall well-being.
